title: Chicago, 1893: Shadows of the White City
premise: The player, assistant to Daniel Burnham, must uncover and stop a conspiracy to sabotage the World's Columbian Exposition before opening day.

playerRoleOptions.name: Burnham's Assistant
playerRoleOptions.description: A trusted aide with access to Burnham's office and the Administration Building. You move in official circles and can question staff without raising suspicion.
playerRoleOptions.perspective: The player is Burnham's assistant. Burnham is the player's superior and may speak to the player directly, give orders, and act as a boss. Write the player as an aide acting on Burnham's authority.

playerRoleOptions.name: Daniel Burnham
playerRoleOptions.description: Director of Works. You built this city from mud and ambition. You have command authority over the fair — but your reputation rides on every decision you make here.
playerRoleOptions.perspective: The player IS Daniel Burnham. Never write 'Burnham says to you' or treat Burnham as an NPC speaking to the player. Narrate from Burnham's point of view. NPCs address the player as 'Mr. Burnham' or 'sir'. Burnham commands — he does not receive orders.

playerRoleOptions.name: Watchman Murphy
playerRoleOptions.description: A night watchman who patrols the freight yards and service corridors. You've seen things the officials haven't noticed. You know this fair from the ground up.
startingKnowledge: knows unofficial routes through the fairgrounds
playerRoleOptions.perspective: The player is Watchman Murphy, a night watchman with no management authority. Burnham is a distant authority figure who should not appear in scenes unless the player explicitly travels to find him. Other workers and guards are the player's peers.

burnhams_assistant.narrative:
Chicago, May 1893. The White City rises from the lakefront mud, its plaster palaces gleaming in the morning haze — ten thousand workers still swarming Jackson Park in the final days before the Exposition opens. But beneath the spectacle, something is wrong. A tension moves through the corridors of the Administration Building that has nothing to do with last-minute preparations.

You are assistant to Daniel Burnham, Director of Works. When he needs eyes he can trust, he sends for you.

This morning, a shipping manifest crossed your desk: electrical equipment, diverted from its declared route. The signature authorizing the change does not match any name on Burnham's staff.

Burnham's office door stands open. He is at his desk, jaw set, a telegraph slip folded in his hand. He looks up.

npcMoments.text: 'Shut the door. Tell me what you found in those papers.'

choices: Show Burnham the mismatched signature on the manifest
Ask Burnham what he already suspects
Request permission to go directly to the freight yards

daniel_burnham.narrative:
Chicago, May 1893. You built this city from swamp and ambition — ten thousand men answering to your hand, four hundred buildings risen from bare ground in two years. The White City is yours. And now, three days from opening, someone is trying to take it from you.

A telegraph arrived before dawn: a consignment of electrical equipment rerouted without your authorization. Someone signed a transfer order in your name. The forgery is competent. That tells you something.

You are Daniel Burnham, Director of Works. Your desk faces the door. Your assistant stands before you with a second document — the shipping manifest. You unfold it.
choices: Study the forged signature on the transfer order
Send for the head of fair security immediately
Go to the freight yards yourself, unannounced

watchman_murphy.narrative:
Chicago, May 1893. Three in the morning and you have been walking the freight yards since midnight — boots wet, lamp low, the smell of lake mud and fresh timber everywhere. You are Watchman Murphy, night patrol. You know every gate, every shadow, every man who belongs here.

That delivery was wrong. You knew it the moment the wagon came through the south gate without papers — four men unloading crates marked with an electrical supplier's stamp, working fast and saying nothing. They were gone before you could get close. But you noted the time. You noted the crate markings. And you saw the man watching from the tree line who did not work for the fair.

Now it is morning. The yards are quiet. The evidence is still here if you move now.
choices: Examine the ground where the crates were unloaded
Check the south gate log to see if the delivery was recorded
Go to the Administration Building and report what you saw

openingSituation: A discrepancy in shipping manifests and an unnerving conversation overheard near the Administration Building suggest that someone is tampering with critical fair infrastructure.
goal: Identify the conspirators, confirm the method of sabotage, and stop it before public disaster or humiliation strikes the fair.

acts.name: Setup
beats: At least one suspect becomes visible

acts.name: Pressure
beats: A second suspect or complication emerges
Sabotage method or target becomes clearer

pressureEvents: A telegraph arrives with contradictory instructions
A guard reports movement near restricted electrical equipment
A newspaper man gets wind of a scandal

winConditions: The true sabotage method is identified
The player prevents or neutralizes the sabotage
Burnham or fair security accepts the player's warning in time
failConditions: Time expires before action is taken
The player accuses the wrong person and loses critical support
The sabotage succeeds sufficiently to damage the fair's opening or reputation
partialSuccessExamples: The immediate sabotage is stopped, but the conspirators escape
The fair opens safely, but scandal reaches the newspapers
Burnham is saved political embarrassment, but the player sacrifices an ally
